description_long: This object is a stylized torii gate, a traditional Japanese structure often found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. It features two robust, vermilion-colored vertical pillars, each supported by additional posts and black bases, giving it a monumental and stable appearance. The top crossbeam is wide and gently curves upward at both ends, painted in a teal color, which is characteristic of many iconic torii gates. Decorative elements include gold square accents on the pillars and black ornamental caps on the supporting posts. At the center of the top beam, there is a hanging plaque with a stylized symbol, adding to its ceremonial significance. The overall design is both functional and symbolic, serving as a spiritual threshold and a prominent historical landmark.
description: A stylized Japanese torii gate with vermilion pillars, teal curved crossbeam, gold accents, and a central hanging plaque. The structure is monumental and richly decorated, evoking traditional Shinto architecture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the teal curved crossbeam, central hanging plaque, and symmetrical vermilion pillars with gold accents.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the teal crossbeam's curvature and the arrangement of the supporting posts and black caps.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the teal crossbeam and vermilion pillars visible, but the central plaque is less prominent.
description_view_3: Top-down rear view, similar to view 1, showing the teal crossbeam and the symmetrical layout of the supporting posts.
